emails.body:
Team,

Quick snapshot for the week:

- Training run 17 completed overnight. Loss curve continues to flatten; validation MAE sitting at 1.3% on the held-out set.
- Urban tile performance still lags rural by ~0.4%, but the gap narrowed this cycle after the new augmentation pipeline kicked in.
- GPU cluster utilization averaged 87% — we’re throughput-bound on the data loader, not compute. Josh is profiling the prefetch queue.
- Next milestone: freeze feature set by COB Friday so we can start the v17 validation sweep.

No blockers. I’ll push the updated learning curves to the shared drive by 1400.

Josh

Thanks, Josh. Good momentum.

I’ll sync with the comms team to keep the external narrative anchored to the projected 1% figure as we approach the Senate brief. Let’s make sure the validation sweep documentation is clean for the deck.

Rebecca

Josh,

Saw the loss curve update — solid progress. With the Senate staff briefing locked for the 15th, I need to confirm the training run schedule supports a defensible 1% error claim.

Are we still on track for the v17 validation sweep to close by the 10th? That gives us a week to bake the briefing deck (v17 baseline) and run the final QA pass.

Also, any word on the data-loader bottleneck? If it’s still limiting epoch time, we may need to ask COL Abreu for a compute rebalance.

James

Josh, James,

GPU utilization holding at 85-90% across the cluster. The prefetch queue refactor Josh mentioned is in staging; early tests show a 12% epoch-time reduction. I’ll promote to prod tonight if the smoke tests pass.

No compute rebalance needed at this stage — we’re within margin. I’ve also authorized the temporary burst allocation for the v17 sweep (200 A100-hours) so the validation run won’t contend with the nightly retrain jobs.

Rebecca, James — I’ll keep you copied on the promotion note.

Jesse

Rebecca,

Convergence notes from the v17 sweep so far:

- Aggregate MAE trending 1.8-2.0% on the full validation set. Rural tiles consistently sub-1%; urban tiles 2.3-2.6%.
- The augmentation pipeline helped, but the domain shift in dense urban canyons is still the dominant error source. We’re seeing systematic under-prediction on multi-story occlusion cases.
- Learning rate schedule plateaued at epoch 42. Early stop would have saved ~18 hrs; I’ll adjust the callback for the production run.

Bottom line: we’re not at 1% aggregate yet. I’m not comfortable representing 1% as a final validated number without at least one more sweep targeting the urban failure modes.

Happy to walk through the per-tile breakdown tomorrow.

Josh

Josh,

Appreciate the candor. Software integration is proceeding on schedule — the inference service passed the latency SLA (p99 < 120ms) and the API contract is frozen.

On the validation gap: the briefing deck (v17) will continue to reference projected performance with the validation timeline noted. We’ll keep the external messaging aligned to the 1% target while the final sweep runs.

Let me know if you need any integration-side instrumentation for the urban-focused sweep.

Rebecca

Josh, Rebecca, Jesse,

Validation is on track per the plan we briefed. The v17 deck is being built now; I’ve already sent the v13 version to Stix for early staff review (slide count matches, no content changes expected).

Still confident the 1% figure is defensible for the Senate brief — the urban gap is known, documented, and the mitigation path is funded. We’ll close the final sweep before the 15th.

James

Josh,

Friday closeout: GPU burst allocation returned to pool, v17 validation sweep kicked off at 0600, briefing deck (v17) with COL Abreu for final review by 1400. No new risks.

See you Monday for the pre-brief sync.

Jesse
